description_long: This barn features a large, rectangular footprint with a prominent, steeply pitched hip roof that extends over the entire structure. The roof is clad in a dark, textured material, likely asphalt shingles, providing a weather-resistant covering. The exterior walls display a distinctive half-timbered pattern, with visible wooden framing and infill panels, evoking a traditional European or Tudor influence. The barn is elevated on sturdy wooden posts, creating open bays along the longer sides, which are likely used for ventilation or access. One short end of the barn includes a main entryway, with a single, wide door and no visible windows. The overall design is utilitarian yet visually striking, suitable for agricultural storage or livestock shelter in a rural or peri-urban setting.
description: A large, half-timbered barn with a steep hip roof, open bays along the sides, and a single main entry door. The structure is elevated on wooden posts and features a traditional, rustic appearance.
description_view_0: The long side of the barn is visible, showing open bays supported by wooden posts and a section of half-timbered wall at one end.
description_view_1: The short end of the barn is shown, highlighting the half-timbered gable and the main entry door, with the hip roof sloping down on all sides.
description_view_2: The opposite long side is visible, featuring a continuous row of open bays beneath the overhanging roof, with no enclosed wall sections.
description_view_3: The other short end is shown, mirroring the half-timbered gable but with a smaller entryway and no large door.
